recipes.title: Kabsa
recipes.description: Saudi national rice dish of long-grain basmati spiced with dried lime and cardamom, topped with tender chicken or lamb.
steps: Brown chicken pieces in oil with chopped onions until golden.
Stir in tomato paste, garlic, cardamom, cinnamon, cumin, and kabsa spice; cook 2 minutes.
Add chopped tomatoes, dried limes pierced with a knife, salt, and 1.2 liters water.
Simmer covered for 35 minutes until chicken is tender; remove chicken and grill or roast 10 minutes to crisp.
Add washed rice to the broth and simmer covered on low heat for 18 minutes.
Toast almonds in oil until golden and plump raisins in a little water.
Mound rice on a platter, top with chicken, and scatter almonds and raisins.

recipes.title: Mandi
recipes.description: Yemeni-Saudi smoky rice and lamb cooked together in an underground tandoor, fragrant with cardamom and saffron.
steps: Rub lamb with cardamom, black lime, salt, and oil; rest 1 hour.
Place lamb on a rack inside a deep covered roasting tray with 500 ml water in the base.
Roast at 160C for 3 hours until very tender, basting once.
Light a piece of charcoal until red, place in a small heatproof bowl in the tray, drizzle with oil, and seal foil tightly for 5 minutes to smoke; remove.
Soak rice 30 minutes; bloom saffron in 3 tablespoons hot water.
Bring 1 liter of the lamb roasting juices to a boil with ghee and salt; add drained rice and saffron.
Simmer covered on lowest heat for 20 minutes until rice is tender.
Plate the rice and arrange the smoked lamb on top with toasted almonds and plumped raisins.

recipes.title: Saleeg
recipes.description: Hejazi creamy white rice porridge cooked in chicken broth with milk and butter, traditionally served with whole chicken.
steps: Simmer chicken with onion, bay leaves, cardamom, salt, and black pepper in 2 liters water for 1 hour.
Strain the broth and reserve the chicken; brush chicken with butter and roast at 200C for 15 minutes to brown.
Add washed rice to 1.2 liters of the broth and simmer 25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Pour in the milk and continue stirring for 15 more minutes until creamy and porridge-like.
Stir in butter and adjust salt.
Spread rice on a platter, set the roasted chicken on top, and serve with shatta sauce.

recipes.title: Jareesh
recipes.description: Najdi crushed wheat porridge slow-cooked with lamb, yogurt, and onion until creamy and rich.
steps: Rinse crushed wheat thoroughly until the water runs clear.
Brown cubed lamb in ghee with chopped onion until caramelized.
Add cardamom, cinnamon, salt, pepper, and 2 liters water; simmer 1 hour until lamb is tender.
Stir in the rinsed wheat and simmer 1.5 hours, stirring often, until very thick and creamy.
Whisk in the yogurt and cook another 10 minutes.
Caramelize sliced onion in ghee until deep brown.
Spoon jareesh into bowls and top with the dark caramelized onions.

recipes.title: Mutabbaq
recipes.description: Saudi stuffed pastry of thin dough filled with spiced minced meat, onion, and egg, fried crisp in golden squares.
steps: Mix flour, salt, and water into a soft dough; knead 8 minutes and rest covered with oil for 1 hour.
Brown minced beef with chopped onion, cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper; cool.
Stir in chopped spring onions, leek, and beaten eggs.
Divide dough into 6 balls; oil generously and stretch each into a paper-thin square.
Spoon filling into the center, fold sides over to form a square parcel.
Pan-fry parcels in shallow oil for 3 minutes per side until deep golden and crisp.
Cut into pieces and serve hot with chili sauce.

recipes.title: Tharid
recipes.description: Saudi peasant dish of spiced lamb and vegetable stew ladled over torn discs of regag bread to soak up the broth.
steps: Brown cubed lamb with chopped onions in oil.
Stir in tomato paste, baharat, and dried limes.
Add chopped tomatoes, salt, and 1.5 liters water; simmer 1 hour.
Add cubed carrots, potatoes, and courgettes and cook 25 minutes more until vegetables are tender.
Tear regag bread into rough pieces and pile in a deep serving bowl.
Ladle the stew over the bread and let it soak for 5 minutes before serving.

recipes.title: Areeka
recipes.description: Saudi dessert of crumbled flatbread mixed with mashed dates and honey, drizzled with ghee for special occasions.
steps: Tear flatbread into small pieces and toast in 60 g of ghee in a wide skillet until golden and crisp.
Crush the toasted bread to a coarse crumb in a food processor.
Warm dates with the remaining ghee and cardamom, mashing into a smooth paste.
Combine the bread crumb with the date paste and knead until evenly mixed and pliable.
Press into a serving bowl and drizzle generously with honey and extra warm ghee.
Serve warm with Arabic coffee.

recipes.title: Mathlouthah
recipes.description: Saudi southern dish of three ingredients: rice, wheat, and lentils slow-simmered with lamb and warm spices.
steps: Brown cubed lamb in ghee with chopped onions.
Stir in tomato paste, cumin, cardamom, and cinnamon; cook 2 minutes.
Add chopped tomatoes, salt, and 2 liters water; simmer 45 minutes.
Stir in rinsed wheat and lentils; cook another 45 minutes.
Add washed rice and continue cooking 25 minutes until everything is tender and the texture is loose porridge.
Adjust salt and add hot water if needed; stir in extra ghee.
Serve hot with caramelized onions on top.

recipes.title: Margoog
recipes.description: Saudi Najdi stew of hand-rolled wheat dough discs simmered with lamb, vegetables, and warm spices.
steps: Mix flour with a pinch of salt and 150 ml water into a stiff dough; rest 30 minutes.
Brown cubed lamb in oil with chopped onions; stir in tomato paste, baharat, and dried limes.
Add chopped tomatoes, salt, and 2 liters water; simmer 45 minutes.
Add cubed carrots, potatoes, and pumpkin; simmer 15 minutes.
Roll the dough thinly and tear into rustic 5 cm discs.
Drop the dough discs into the simmering stew and cook 15 minutes until the dough is tender and the broth has thickened.
Adjust seasoning and serve in deep bowls.

recipes.title: Shakshuka
recipes.description: Eggs poached in spiced tomato and pepper sauce, a beloved breakfast across Saudi and the Levant.
steps: Sauté chopped onion and pepper in olive oil for 8 minutes until very soft.
Add minced garlic, cumin, paprika, and chili flakes; cook 30 seconds.
Stir in chopped tomatoes and salt and simmer 15 minutes until thickened.
Make 6 wells in the sauce and crack an egg into each.
Cover and cook on low heat for 6 minutes until whites are set but yolks remain runny.
Scatter chopped parsley and serve straight from the pan with bread.

recipes.title: Harees
recipes.description: Saudi Ramadan dish of wheat berries slow-cooked with chicken or lamb until smooth, beaten into a creamy porridge.
steps: Soak wheat berries overnight in plenty of water.
Simmer drained wheat with chicken, onion, cardamom, salt, and 2.5 liters water for 3 hours, skimming foam.
Remove the bones and shred the chicken into the pot.
Beat the mixture with a wooden spoon for 20 minutes until smooth, stringy, and porridge-like.
Stir in cinnamon and adjust salt.
Spoon into a wide bowl and pour melted ghee generously over the top.

recipes.title: Luqaimat
recipes.description: Crispy Saudi yeast-risen dumplings deep fried until golden, drizzled with date syrup and sesame seeds.
steps: Bloom saffron in 2 tablespoons hot water.
Mix flour, salt, cardamom, yeast, sugar, yogurt, saffron water, and warm water into a thick batter.
Cover and rest 1.5 hours until bubbly and doubled.
Heat oil to 170C.
Pinch small balls of batter from a wet hand and drop into the oil; fry 4 minutes until deep golden, turning often.
Drain on paper, then immediately drizzle with date syrup and shower with sesame seeds.
Serve hot.
